project: OpenTabs Platform — RetroUI Exact Match Audit
description: Fix every CSS token, color value, shadow, and hover effect that deviates from the RetroUI source code. The standard is pixel-identical to RetroUI's app/global.css and component source files.

userStories.title: Fix --muted light mode value in side panel and docs CSS
userStories.description: As a user, I want the light mode muted color to exactly match RetroUI so that the design system is pixel-identical.
userStories.notes: RetroUI app/global.css :root defines --muted: #d5d5d5. Both our files currently have #aeaeae. Change line 46 in styles.css and line 52 in docs global.css. Simple find and replace of the hex value.

userStories.title: Fix --border dark mode value in side panel and docs CSS
userStories.description: As a user, I want the dark mode border color to exactly match RetroUI so borders render identically in dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: No other file in docs/src/ or side-panel/ contains --border: #3a3a3a in a dark mode block
userStories.notes: RetroUI app/global.css .dark defines --border: #5c5c5c. Both our files have #3a3a3a. In side-panel styles.css it's line 72. In docs global.css it's around line 70. Change the hex value in both .dark blocks.

userStories.title: Replace hardcoded rgba animation color with RetroUI theme token in side panel styles
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CSS animations to use theme tokens instead of hardcoded rgba values so they adapt to theme changes.
acceptanceCriteria: The @keyframes tool-pulse in styles.css uses color-mix or var(--primary) or var(--accent) instead of rgba(245, 158, 11, ...)
The animation still produces a subtle pulse effect
No hardcoded rgba(...) color values remain in styles.css (the pulse-dot animation using opacity is fine since it doesn't reference a color)
userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css lines 99-106, the tool-pulse keyframe uses rgba(245, 158, 11, 0) and rgba(245, 158, 11, 0.05). This is a hardcoded amber color. Replace with var(--primary) at low opacity using color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 5%, transparent) for the 50% keyframe, and transparent for 0%/100%. Example: 0%, 100% { background-color: transparent; } 50% { background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 5%, transparent); }

userStories.title: Fix header shadow to use RetroUI shadow pattern in both side panel and docs
userStories.description: As a user, I want the header bottom shadow to follow RetroUI's shadow convention (equal X and Y offsets) rather than a non-RetroUI Y-only pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Side panel Header.tsx uses shadow-sm (2px 2px 0 0 var(--border)) or shadow (3px 3px 0 0 var(--border)) instead of shadow-[0_3px_0_0_var(--border)]
Docs Header.astro uses box-shadow matching a RetroUI shadow token (e.g., 2px 2px 0 0 var(--border)) instead of 0 3px 0 0 var(--border)
No 0 Npx 0 0 shadow pattern exists anywhere in the codebase (except as part of border-shadow utilities)
userStories.notes: RetroUI shadows always have equal X and Y offsets: 1px 1px, 2px 2px, 3px 3px, etc. The header uses 0 3px 0 0 which is a Y-only offset not present in RetroUI. In side panel Header.tsx line 5, replace shadow-[0_3px_0_0_var(--border)] with shadow-sm (which is 2px 2px 0 0). In docs Header.astro line 12, replace box-shadow: 0 3px 0 0 var(--border) with box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 0 var(--border). The visual difference is minimal but this makes it exactly match RetroUI's shadow definitions.

userStories.title: Add RetroUI hover pattern to Footer link in side panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want the footer Feedback link to use RetroUI's standard link hover effect (underline with primary decoration).
acceptanceCriteria: Footer.tsx link has hover:underline underline-offset-2 decoration-primary classes matching RetroUI Text 'a' variant
The transition-colors class is retained for smooth transition
userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx line 8, the link has hover:text-foreground transition-colors but is missing RetroUI's standard link hover pattern. RetroUI Text component 'a' variant uses: hover:underline underline-offset-2 decoration-primary. Add these classes to the existing className. Keep hover:text-foreground and transition-colors. The full className should be: hover:text-foreground hover:underline underline-offset-2 decoration-primary transition-colors

userStories.title: Fix light mode --sl-color-gray values to use RetroUI tokens in docs CSS
userStories.description: As a user viewing the docs in light mode, I want Starlight gray variables to map to RetroUI tokens instead of hardcoded hex values.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/src/styles/global.css light mode block, --sl-color-gray-5 uses var(--muted) instead of #e5e5e5
In docs/src/styles/global.css light mode block, --sl-color-gray-6 uses var(--card) instead of #f0f0f0
In docs/src/styles/global.css light mode block, --sl-color-gray-7 uses var(--background) instead of #f8f8f8
No hardcoded gray hex values (#e5e5e5, #f0f0f0, #f8f8f8) remain in Starlight overrides
userStories.notes: In docs/src/styles/global.css around lines 159-161, the light mode Starlight overrides have: --sl-color-gray-5: #e5e5e5; --sl-color-gray-6: #f0f0f0; --sl-color-gray-7: #f8f8f8. These should map to RetroUI tokens. Map gray-5 to var(--muted), gray-6 to var(--card), gray-7 to var(--background). Also add --sl-color-gray-7 to the dark mode block mapping it to var(--background) so Starlight doesn't fall back to its own default.

userStories.title: Fix docs inline code border-width from 1px to 2px to match RetroUI border convention
userStories.description: As a user reading docs, I want inline code elements to use RetroUI's consistent border-2 pattern rather than a thinner 1px border.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/src/styles/global.css the .sl-markdown-content :not(pre) > code rule uses border: 2px solid var(--border) instead of 1px
userStories.notes: In docs/src/styles/global.css around line 223, the inline code rule has border: 1px solid var(--border). RetroUI uses border-2 (2px) consistently on all elements — Input, Card, Accordion, Alert, Badge etc. Change 1px to 2px to match. The rule is: .sl-markdown-content :not(pre) > code { border: 1px solid var(--border); ... } Change to: border: 2px solid var(--border);

userStories.title: Fix docs table cell border-width from 1px to 2px to match RetroUI Table component
userStories.description: As a user reading docs, I want table cells to use RetroUI's border-2 convention rather than 1px borders.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/src/styles/global.css the .sl-markdown-content :is(th, td) rule uses border: 2px solid var(--border) instead of 1px
userStories.notes: In docs/src/styles/global.css around line 233, table cells use border: 1px solid var(--border). RetroUI Table component uses border-2 on the wrapper (Table.tsx: 'w-full caption-bottom text-sm border-2 shadow-lg'). For consistency with the RetroUI border-2 convention, change cell borders to 2px. The rule is: .sl-markdown-content :is(th, td) { border: 1px solid var(--border); ... } Change to: border: 2px solid var(--border);
